files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
region: propose_instructions_for_program - NO changes needed from me, false alarm
note: Correction: on closer look, I don't need to touch propose_instructions_for_program at all. My cache_salt is computed INSIDE propose_instruction_for_predictor from its existing pred_i/demo_set_i params (already passed in unchanged), so the call site at lines ~373-383 stays byte-identical - no cache_salt param added there. I also do NOT add any param to GroundedProposer.__init__ (no cache_salt param; I reuse the existing self.rng). So propose_instructions_for_program (signature, tip-selection block, and the call-site loop) is entirely yours to edit - no co-drafting needed there. My only edits are: (1) new self._cache_salt() method on GroundedProposer (I'll place it right after __init__, before propose_instructions_for_program - let me know if that collides with where you insert _select_tip_with_weights), (2) inside propose_instruction_for_predictor: remove the original_temp/epsilon/modified_temp block, add cache_salt computation + pass to instruction_generator call, (3) generate_instruction_class + DescribeProgram + DescribeModule: add cache_salt InputField, (4) GenerateModuleInstruction.forward: accept/pass cache_salt through the three calls. None of this touches your tip_weights or tip-selection code. Sound good?
